text: Relative to the S&P 500 benchmark (SPY), Amazon has demonstrated modest outperformance with a 30-day price return of 4.25% compared to the benchmark's 3.58%, generating an excess return of 0.67%. This relative strength ahead of earnings reflects positive analyst sentiment, including UBS lifting its price target on the stock. Market commentary suggests Amazon is well-positioned to benefit from rising demand for chips and AI tools as the broader technology sector increases AI spending. The company's aggressive R&D investment at 16.20% of revenue underscores its commitment to innovation across cloud infrastructure and AI capabilities. However, this momentum should be viewed cautiously given the concentrated earnings period for Magnificent Seven stocks and broader market uncertainty surrounding upcoming economic data.
